pick.rationale: North Carolina holds a meaningful edge in adjusted efficiency — KenPom #29 (AdjEM +20.86) and Torvik #27 versus VCU's KenPom #46 (AdjEM +16.96) and Torvik #51 — built against elite ACC competition that the A-10 cannot replicate, and the Tar Heels benefit from a quasi-home crowd at Bon Secours Wellness Arena in Greenville, SC. However, the loss of All-ACC First Team freshman Caleb Wilson (season-ending broken thumb) is a severe blow that caps UNC's ceiling and explains why the market opened them as only a 2.5-point favorite — an unusually tight line for a 6-vs-11 matchup. VCU enters on a hot streak as back-to-back A-10 Tournament champions (27-7), with a disruptive HAVOC-style defense that historically creates tournament chaos and has been flagged by multiple analysts as one of the most credible upset scenarios in the bracket. UNC's schedule-strength advantage, superior efficiency margin, and home-crowd factor narrowly tip this pick to the Tar Heels, but Wilson's absence makes this a genuine coin-flip with real upset risk.

reasoningStep.title: North Carolina vs. VCU — Round of 64 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: UNC holds a ~4-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over VCU across both KenPom and Torvik, but star freshman Caleb Wilson (All-ACC First Team) is out for the season with a broken thumb, narrowing the gap significantly. VCU enters as back-to-back A-10 Tournament champions on a hot streak, and the 2.5-point betting line reflects a near-coin-flip. UNC's ACC schedule strength, efficiency edge, and quasi-home crowd in Greenville, SC tip the pick narrowly to the Tar Heels.
evidence: UNC: KenPom #29 (AdjEM +20.86), Torvik #27 (AdjOE 121.5, AdjDE 100.0). VCU: KenPom #46 (AdjEM +16.96), Torvik #51 (AdjOE 118.9, AdjDE 103.8). Roughly 4-point efficiency gap favoring UNC.

Caleb Wilson (All-ACC First Team freshman) is out for the season with a broken right thumb. UNC went 5-3 in 8 games without him, and multiple analysts noted this caps their ceiling heading into the tournament.
VCU won back-to-back A-10 Tournament titles, beating Dayton 70-62 in the 2026 final to finish 27-7, entering the tournament on a hot streak with a disruptive HAVOC-style defensive identity.
UNC opened as only a 2.5-point favorite (total 155.5), reflecting the market's acknowledgment of Wilson's absence. The Athletic listed VCU among the 10 most likely upsets in the bracket.
Game played at Bon Secours Wellness Arena in Greenville, SC — geographically close to Chapel Hill, giving UNC a meaningful crowd advantage that partially offsets VCU's momentum edge.

Contest Click to expand picks panel Click to open Sharp AI North Carolina vs. VCU: NCAA Tournament Picks & Odds by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 The 2026 NCAA Tournament is officially here! The March Madness Bracket is set, and first-round NCAA Tournament matchups are in place. It’s time to make your picks and predictions for the first round of the 2026 NCAA Tournament! We’re here to help as we’ll have picks and predictions for each of the first round 2026 NCAA Tournament games. Here are our NCAA Tournament picks and predictions for North Carolina vs. VCU. NCAA Tournament Odds NCAA Tournament Pick’Em Contest NCAA Tournament March Madness Bracket Optimizer NCAA Tournament Betting Trends 2026 NCAA Tournament Picks & Predictions: North Carolina vs. VCU Let’s dive into our top NCAA Tournament picks and predictions for this opening-round matchup. 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview: North Carolina vs. VCU North Carolina 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview Everything about North Carolina’s tournament outlook is limited by Caleb Wilson’s injury. The First Team All-ACC freshman was having a historic year before surgery ended his season in February, and without him, the Tar Heels enter the tournament with consecutive losses and major questions. Without Wilson in the lineup, this squad drops from 21st overall to 32nd, per Hoop Explorer, with the defense dropping from 20th to 40th. EvanMiya dropped the Tar Heels outside his top 45 without Wilson. Since Feb. 14, UNC ranks 38th on Bart Torvik. Wilson is obviously a big loss. Henri Veesaar is the player who has to fill the void. The junior center put up 28 points and 17 rebounds in the loss to Clemson in the ACC Tournament and is a legitimate NBA-caliber interior presence who creates matchup problems by being able to score in the post, step out on the perimeter, and rebound at an elite rate. He and Seth Trimble are the most reliable contributors left. The rest of the supporting cast is a concern. The bench has combined for single-digit scoring in each of the last two games, and the second unit has not given Hubert Davis anything meaningful to work with when the starters are struggling. The three-point defense is the other genuine problem. UNC ranks outside the top 200 nationally in opponent three-point percentage, and Clemson exploited it by shooting nearly 50% from deep in the ACC quarterfinals. Any opponent that can spread the floor and shoot will find open looks against this scheme. The Tar Heels also rank 344th in field goal rate allowed with few forced turnovers, so opponents will get shots up most

result.text: UNC opens NCAA Tournament without Caleb Wilson vs. surging VCU :: WRAL.com UNC opens NCAA Tournament without Caleb Wilson vs. surging VCU No. 6 seed North Carolina begins its NCAA Tournament run Thursday against No. 11 seed VCU while adjusting to life without star freshman Caleb Wilson. The Tar Heels have gone 5-3 since Wilson’s season-ending thumb injury. Posted 3/17/2026, 12:51:55 AM Updated 3/17/2026, 4:37:53 PM Related UNC sports UNC basketball Hubert Davis college basketball NCAA Tournament March Madness Selection Sunday Hubert Davis on VCU: We'll be ready for the challenge By Mark Bergin, WRAL senior multiplatform producer North Carolina will have somewhat of a homecourt advantage in the opening weekend of the 2026 NCAA Tournament if the Tar Heels can manage without star freshman Caleb Wilson. No. 6 seed UNC (24-8) plays No. 11 seed VCU (27-7) at 6:50 p.m. ET Thursday at Bon Secours Wellness Arena in Greenville, South Carolina. The South Region game airs on TNT. Other WRAL Top Stories Wilson, an All-ACC first-team selection, is out for the season after breaking his right thumb. Wilson injured the thumb while working back from a left hand fracture. Wilson's last game was on Feb. 20. UNC is 5-3 in eight games without Wilson. "Our team will move forward," UNC coach Hubert Davis said of Wilson's injury on March 7 after the regular-season finale against Duke. Davis added, "Obviously, there's tremendous sadness for him. I've talked about how special ... obviously he's a special player, but just how remarkable of a kid and a teammate he is and the passion that he has for his teammates, for North Carolina for being on the floor, for playing in games like this. It was a dream for him to play in the ACC [Tournament] and the NCAA Tournament, and my heart is broken that he won't be able to do that." The UNC-VCU winner will play the winner of the Illinois-Penn game on Saturday in Greenville, South Carolina. The other teams in the South Region include No. 1 seed Florida, No. 2 seed Houston, No. 4 seed Nebraska, No. 5 seed Vanderbilt, No. 7 seed Saint Mary’s, No. 8 seed Clemson, No. 9 seed Iowa, No. 10 seed Texas A&M, No. 12 seed McNeese, No. 13 seed Troy, No. 15 seed, Idaho, No. 16 seed Lehigh and No. 16 seed Prairie View A&M. UNC enters the NCAA Tournament on a two-game losing streak. Thursday marks the Tar Heels’ 55th appearance in the NCAA Tournament, the second most all-time. It is the fourth NCAA Tournament appearance in five seasons under head coach Hubert Davis, who has led the Tar Heels to an 8-3 record in the NCAA Tournament. The Tar Heels fell to Duke in the regular-season finale and lost 80-79 to Clemson in the ACC quarterfinals . Meanwhile, the Rams have won 16 of their last 17 games. It includes a three-game sweep of Duquesne, Saint Joseph's and Dayton to win the A-10 title this past weekend in Pittsburgh. NCAA selection committee chair Keith Gill said the committee discussed Wilson's injury and how it treated UNC. "We spent a lot

McNeese, VCU and High Point: We’re sizing you up for glass slippers. UNC, Vanderbilt and Wisconsin? You might need some Rolaids. Advertisement Welcome to Year 20 of Bracket Breakers! If you’ve been following us this whole time, you know to expect a whole lot of upset-predicting math and even more puns in the coming days. If you’re new to the scene, here’s what to expect: We will provide deep, regional breakdowns of every first-round matchup with a difference of at least five seeds, powered by our upset-seeking model, Slingshot. You can read a detailed explanation of how the process works here . Want a closer look? Dig into our Definitive Guide to First-Round Upsets for in-depth explanations as to why our model projects this year’s upsets the way it does. But for now, here’s a quick look at the 10 most likely first-round upsets according to our model. No. 5 Vanderbilt Commodores vs. No. 12 McNeese Cowboys Upset Chance: 41.2 percent Last week, we identified the Cowboys as our model’s second-favorite potential Bracket Breaker . And since Santa Clara (our top choice) ended up seeded 10th, putting them in a game without a five-seed difference, McNeese now tops our chart. The Cowboys lead the country in forcing turnovers (24.9 percent of opponents’ possessions), and they grab offensive boards at a top-50 rate, which makes Slingshot positively swoon. Contest Click to expand picks panel Click to open Sharp AI North Carolina vs. VCU: NCAA Tournament Picks & Odds by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 The 2026 NCAA Tournament is officially here! The March Madness Bracket is set, and first-round NCAA Tournament matchups are in place. It’s time to make your picks and predictions for the first round of the 2026 NCAA Tournament! We’re here to help as we’ll have picks and predictions for each of the first round 2026 NCAA Tournament games. Here are our NCAA Tournament picks and predictions for North Carolina vs. VCU. NCAA Tournament Odds NCAA Tournament Pick’Em Contest NCAA Tournament March Madness Bracket Optimizer NCAA Tournament Betting Trends 2026 NCAA Tournament Picks & Predictions: North Carolina vs. VCU Let’s dive into our top NCAA Tournament picks and predictions for this opening-round matchup. 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview: North Carolina vs. VCU North Carolina 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview Everything about North Carolina’s tournament outlook is limited by Caleb Wilson’s injury. The First Team All-ACC freshman was having a historic year before surgery ended his season in February, and without him, the Tar Heels enter the tournament with consecutive losses and major questions. Without Wilson in the lineup, this squad drops from 21st overall to 32nd, per Hoop Explorer, with the defense dropping from 20th to 40th. EvanMiya dropped the Tar Heels outside his top 45 without Wilson. Since Feb. 14, UNC ranks 38th on Bart Torvik. Wilson is obviously a big loss. Henri Veesaar is the player who has to fill the void. The junior center put up 28 points and 17 rebounds in the loss to Clemson in the ACC Tournament and is a legitimate NBA-caliber interior presence who creates matchup problems by being able to score in the post, step out on the perimeter, and rebound at an elite rate. He and Seth Trimble are the most reliable contributors left. The rest of the supporting cast is a concern. The bench has combined for single-digit scoring in each of the last two games, and the second unit has not given Hubert Davis anything meaningful to work with when the starters are struggling. The three-point defense is the other genuine problem. UNC ranks outside the top 200 nationally in opponent three-point percentage, and Clemson exploited it by shooting nearly 50% from deep in the ACC quarterfinals. Any opponent that can spread the floor and shoot will find open looks against this scheme. The Tar Heels also rank 344th in field goal rate allowed with few forced turnovers, so opponents will get shots up most

UNC opens NCAA Tournament without Caleb Wilson vs. surging VCU :: WRAL.com UNC opens NCAA Tournament without Caleb Wilson vs. surging VCU No. 6 seed North Carolina begins its NCAA Tournament run Thursday against No. 11 seed VCU while adjusting to life without star freshman Caleb Wilson. The Tar Heels have gone 5-3 since Wilson’s season-ending thumb injury. Posted 3/17/2026, 12:51:55 AM Updated 3/17/2026, 4:37:53 PM Related UNC sports UNC basketball Hubert Davis college basketball NCAA Tournament March Madness Selection Sunday Hubert Davis on VCU: We'll be ready for the challenge By Mark Bergin, WRAL senior multiplatform producer North Carolina will have somewhat of a homecourt advantage in the opening weekend of the 2026 NCAA Tournament if the Tar Heels can manage without star freshman Caleb Wilson. No. 6 seed UNC (24-8) plays No. 11 seed VCU (27-7) at 6:50 p.m. ET Thursday at Bon Secours Wellness Arena in Greenville, South Carolina. The South Region game airs on TNT. Other WRAL Top Stories Wilson, an All-ACC first-team selection, is out for the season after breaking his right thumb. Wilson injured the thumb while working back from a left hand fracture. Wilson's last game was on Feb. 20. UNC is 5-3 in eight games without Wilson. "Our team will move forward," UNC coach Hubert Davis said of Wilson's injury on March 7 after the regular-season finale against Duke. Davis added, "Obviously, there's tremendous sadness for him. I've talked about how special ... obviously he's a special player, but just how remarkable of a kid and a teammate he is and the passion that he has for his teammates, for North Carolina for being on the floor, for playing in games like this. It was a dream for him to play in the ACC [Tournament] and the NCAA Tournament, and my heart is broken that he won't be able to do that." The UNC-VCU winner will play the winner of the Illinois-Penn game on Saturday in Greenville, South Carolina. The other teams in the South Region include No. 1 seed Florida, No. 2 seed Houston, No. 4 seed Nebraska, No. 5 seed Vanderbilt, No. 7 seed Saint Mary’s, No. 8 seed Clemson, No. 9 seed Iowa, No. 10 seed Texas A&M, No. 12 seed McNeese, No. 13 seed Troy, No. 15 seed, Idaho, No. 16 seed Lehigh and No. 16 seed Prairie View A&M. UNC enters the NCAA Tournament on a two-game losing streak. Thursday marks the Tar Heels’ 55th appearance in the NCAA Tournament, the second most all-time. It is the fourth NCAA Tournament appearance in five seasons under head coach Hubert Davis, who has led the Tar Heels to an 8-3 record in the NCAA Tournament. The Tar Heels fell to Duke in the regular-season finale and lost 80-79 to Clemson in the ACC quarterfinals . Meanwhile, the Rams have won 16 of their last 17 games. It includes a three-game sweep of Duquesne, Saint Joseph's and Dayton to win the A-10 title this past weekend in Pittsburgh. NCAA selection committee chair Keith Gill said the committee discussed Wilson's injury and how it treated UNC. "We spent a lot
